title: Object 54
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Object 54: "Mailboxes"}}{{VoidedTheme}} {{Blurimage|align=right|caption=A typical example of Object 54, found in Level 9. Image brightened for viewing convenience.|file=mailbox2.jpg}} {{Background|file=mailbox2.jpg}} == Description == '''Object 54''', commonly referred to as "'''Mailboxes'''", is a type of anomalous postal container. It is usually found in indoor or urban levels of the Backrooms. Most instances of '''Object 54''' manifest as simple metal containers mounted by the side of roads or other pathways, often near houses or doors, in building lobbies, or other locations where postal boxes would commonly be found in [[The Frontrooms]]. The appearance, condition, and style of '''Object 54''' varies widely from instance to instance. Many '''Mailboxes''' appear to be small wooden, metal or plastic pouches mounted on posts made of wood or metal, similar to Frontrooms mailboxes. Others may appear as hollow spaces cut in small, ornate stone structures and sealed with small doors. A few appear as traditional large blue metal containers locked by key, similar to traditional postal containers placed in public spaces. On rare occasions, '''Object 54''' may resemble large containers such as shipping boxes, safes or even vaults. The primary anomalous property of '''Object 54''' is its ability to transport objects placed inside it across levels. The most common way (that is, the method used when the object takes the form of a normal mailbox) to transport items via '''Object 54''' is to place the items inside, close the lid, and perform the action that traditionally indicates that mail is in the box for a postal worker to pick up, usually raising a flag on one side of the mailbox. Nothing will immediately happen, and opening the lid will reveal the items inside, unchanged. However, after a period of time which varies from mailbox to mailbox, but is typically 4-16 hours, a mechanical noise (commonly described as whirring sounds followed by a "ka-chunk") will emanate from inside the mailbox, and the flag will lower itself to its original position. Opening the mailbox afterward will reveal an empty interior. After 6-72 hours, depending on the mailbox, the items transported will arrive at their destination, deposited either in enclosed locations of other levels, in non-anomalous mailboxes, or in other instances of '''Object 54''' (see Variants below for more information on destinations). An experiment performed that involved "mailing" 5 active radio transmitters through an instance of '''Object 54''' found in [[Level 4.3]] revealed several insights into the travel undergone by "mailed" objects. As soon as the "mailing" sound was heard outside the mailbox, the radio transmitters ceased transmitting. However, over the next 36 hours, intermittent radio signals matching those of the "mailed" transmitters were [[Phenomenon 13|detected at frequencies]] of 4 MHz, 1 MHz, and 154 MHz. It is suspected that the transmitters were "shipped through" Levels 4, 1, possibly the Hub, and Level 154. When the transmitters began broadcasting consistent signals again, it was found that they were transmitting on a frequency of 772 MHz. A subsequent expedition (thoroughly equipped with [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and psi-resistant gear) into [[Level 772]] found all 5 transmitters in a non-anomalous desk drawer. {{Blurimage|align=right|file=mailboxlevel10.jpg|caption=An instance of Object 54, found outside a barn in Level 10. This mailbox connects to a drawer in a cabin in Level 461.}} == Usage == '''Object 54''' has been used by a wide variety of groups since its discovery, including the [[M.E.G.]], [[N.A.X.O.S.]], the [[Backroom Colonists]], the [[Another Dimension Foundation|A. D. F.]], and [[Architect Industries]]. Typically, large mailboxes are used to transport supplies, materials, physical documents, and other items across levels without requiring the items to be no-clipped through levels and without needing [[Object 9|gate-carvers]]. Mailboxes have been used to supply small but necessary items such as [[Object 1|Almond Water]] or medical supplies to a variety of colonies, including colonies in [[Level 1]], [[Level 5]], and [[Level 87]]. Due to inherent difficulty of shipping supplies to colonies using average-sized mailboxes, the major groups mostly use large mailboxes. A few mailboxes are portable enough to be moved to locations that are more convenient for their usage. Most, however, are fixed in place in the ground or floor, typically via concrete or deep post burial, though sometimes by anomalous forces. Forcibly removing fixed mailboxes from their locations almost always causes them to lose their anomalous properties. However, portable mailboxes can be moved to other levels while retaining their transportational capabilities. These mailboxes are quite rare and are of high value to groups and colonies, for both logistical purposes and research purposes. Smaller mailboxes are of lesser value, as they are only large enough to transport letters, a few bottles of liquid, small weapons, trinkets, or other small packages. They are more common than large mailboxes, and are slightly more likely to be portable and/or to link to other instances of '''Object 54''' than large mailboxes are. [[Architect Industries]] is currently undergoing a collaborative research project with [[Another Dimension Foundation]] in an attempt to reverse-engineer '''Object 54'''. They have produced several prototypes; however, none of them are yet capable of functioning consistently and reliably without anomalous support from a Macroformer. == Locations == '''Object 54''' is usually found in indoor or urban levels. It was first discovered in [[Level 9]], and instances of it are more common in that level than in any other. However, the abundance of non-anomalous mailboxes in Level 9, combined with '''Object 54''' being indistinguishable from ordinary mailboxes, makes them difficult to find. Since the transportation takes time to "set up" and get underway, wanderers cannot tell whether the items they are attempting to transport to other levels are actually in '''Object 54''' or are simply being stuffed in non-anomalous mailboxes. Going back to check whether the items were transported later is possible; however, it is very difficult due to Level 9's [[Phenomenon 14|shifting geometry]]. This, along with the high entity count of Level 9, delayed the discovery of '''Object 54''' for a long time. '''Object 54''' can also be found more rarely in [[Level 11]]. In this level, it typically takes the form of larger, gray, metal cabinet-style boxes that sit in corners inside building lobbies. Very rarely, post office box variations can be found here (see Variants below). '''Object 54''' has also been reported to appear as mail slots in fancy hotel rooms in [[Level 19]], as large corner boxes in empty areas of [[Level 20]], and as small drop-in public mailboxes in corners of the Empty Mall in [[Level 330]]. == Variants == Instances of '''Object 54''' occur in a wide variety of colors, forms, shapes, materials, and sizes. '''Object 54''' has been found in wooden, metal, and plastic forms of many different colors, including brown, gray, white, black, red, blue, yellow, and green. The apparent architectural design of mailboxes is also highly varied. Some mailboxes are simple and minimalist, while others are extremely ornately designed. Some are carved with old-fashioned natural elements like leaves, flowers, and branches. Some are molded with modern, surreal designs that employ a varied color palette. The destination of items transported by '''Object 54''' varies widely from mailbox to mailbox. Some mailboxes (about 25%) transport their objects to a random location in a certain level. Sometimes the random location varies each time the mailbox is used, and a few mailboxes even send their contents to different levels each time. Such mailboxes are considered to be "defective" or "excessively random" and are usually not valued highly. Some mailboxes (about 55%) transport their contents to a consistent location each time, such as a drawer, a secluded corner of a storage room, or a non-anomalous mailbox. A few mailboxes (about 20%) transport their contents to another instance of '''Object 54''', which in turn "ships" it's contents back to the first mailbox. Such "linked" mailboxes typically "ship" their contents at the same time, and later receive content from each other at the same time as well.
{{Collapse|initial='''> Open Credits'''|final='''Close Credits'''|body= '''Author''': [[User:Lightseeker12|Lightseeker]]
My first object! I decided to keep it simple for starters.}}
